label: vec_reset_to_boot
notes: Reset vector: DI; JMP 0x0180.

label: vec_ei_reti_stub
notes: Short EI; RETI stub.

label: vec_irq_to_0978
notes: Interrupt/vector stub to 0x0978.

label: vec_irq_to_gate_f000_input_capture
notes: Interrupt/vector stub to 0x0582; 0x0060 also jumps here.

label: vec_irq_to_print_engine_dispatch
notes: Interrupt/vector stub to 0x0668.

label: vec_irq_to_rxb_receive
notes: Interrupt/vector stub to 0x05E2.

label: rom_signature_pbls
notes: Bytes decode as ASCII-like PBLS signature at both 0x0070 and external-check address 0x4000.

label: calt_table_or_fast_services
notes: Dense target area for CALT calls 0x80-0xBE; linear disassembly is not enough to name each entry yet.

label: boot_reset_entry
notes: Initializes SP/MM/V register page, clears RAM/windows, checks external PROM signature, then enters initialization.

label: post_external_prom_boot_init
notes: Boot continuation after optional external PROM check; calls mechanism and DIP/init helpers.

label: startup_sample_gate_f000_status
notes: Boot reads F000 between F2/F1/NMI tests before deriving initial status and switch state.

label: startup_decode_f003_status_bits
notes: Reads F003 and maps active-low bits into VV0A/VV0B startup status fields.

label: init_runtime_state
notes: Clears EE/EF state blocks, seeds print/page pointers, initializes mode variables.

label: init_cpu_ports_timers_gate_array
notes: Writes EOM, PA/PB/PC, MCC/MA/MB/MC, TMM/ETMM, F004, F005, F003.

label: memclear_hl_bc
notes: Stores 0x00 through memory using HL and BC loop.

label: probe_cg_ram_window_set
notes: Calls 0x04B8 with bank-like values 0x80,0x00,0x40 and stores feature flags.

label: probe_8000_window_bank
notes: Uses CALT 0x00B4 then probes 0x8000 window contents.

label: probe_8000_window_pattern
notes: Writes/reads 0x8000 and scans window; likely RAM/CG/bank probe.

label: checksum_8000_page
notes: Sums bytes starting at 0x8000.

label: isr_gate_f000_input_capture_buffer
notes: Reads F000 through the gate-array register window, stores the byte into the EE20 input buffer, restores F002, RETI. Candidate parallel-port data path.

label: isr_rxb_host_receive_buffer
notes: Reads CPU RXB, checks ER, stores received byte into the shared EE20 input buffer while temporarily changing F002.

label: isr_print_engine_dispatch
notes: Large RETI routine dispatching on VV37/VV61 flags; calls motion/print helpers and updates timers.

label: paper_feed_pb2_release_delay_state
notes: FE1 tail-exit state reached when VV37 has bit 0x10 set. Changes VV37 to 0x20, calls 0x540D, and for command feed releases PB2 high/hold because the 0x540D state selector falls through to EF60=00 and then 0x5498.

label: arm_head_f005_burst_output
notes: Writes F004=0xC0, sets alternate-register BC=F005 and DE/EA pointers from EF75/EF79, programs TM1/TMM, and re-enters print timer flow. Strong candidate head-fire burst setup.

label: carriage_gate_array_tm_pulse
notes: Writes MB=0x03 and pulses PC bit 7 low/high with delay slots. Service manual Figure 2-34 identifies CPU CO1/PC7 as the E01A05KA carriage gate-array TM input; FE1 calls this when VV62=0.

label: paper_feed_pb_bits_3_4_phase_update_candidate
notes: Chooses phase rotation by VV61 bit 0, calls 0x0953/0x095F, then updates position/state through 0x54A0/0x54C9/0x5538. Service manual identifies PB3/PB4 as paper-feed phase A/B and C/D signals.

label: rotate_pb_bits_3_4_phase_positive
notes: Rotates VV16 right with wrap, sets EA=+1, then writes VV16 bits 0x18 into PB bits 0x18 through 0x096A.

label: rotate_pb_bits_3_4_phase_negative
notes: Rotates VV16 left with wrap, sets EA=-1, then writes VV16 bits 0x18 into PB bits 0x18 through 0x096A.

label: write_pb_bits_3_4_stepper_phase_outputs
notes: Stores the new VV16 phase and maps VV16 & 0x18 directly onto PB & 0x18 while preserving other PB bits. 0x18 is a mask, not a pin name; service-manual paper-feed phase lines PB3/PB4 correspond to these mask bits if bit numbering is zero-based.

label: isr_head_f005_burst_transfer_reload
notes: Alternate-register ISR writes three bytes to BC, which 0x08D0 presets to F005; direction follows VV61 bit 0, then reloads ETM0 from ECNT+EE3A.

label: read_next_host_input_byte
notes: Blocking consumer for the EE20/EE22 input ring: waits for EE1E pending count, reads one byte from EE22 under F002=0xC0, advances EE22, decrements EE1E, and returns byte in A.

label: disable_f001_irq_or_strobe
notes: DI; clears F001 bits with NOP delays; masks MKL; similar sequence appears in interrupt paths.

label: read_next_host_input_byte_7bit
notes: CALT helper for 7-bit command parameters: calls 0x0A0B then masks A with 0x7F.

label: read_next_host_input_word
notes: CALT helper: reads two host input bytes through 0x0A0B and returns them as a 16-bit value in HL.

label: write_bank_register_f002
notes: Single helper: MOV ($F002),A; RET.

label: line_or_buffer_bounds_check
notes: Uses EFBF, EE4C, EE5C/EE5E and comparisons; likely print-buffer bounds logic.

label: scan_ff00_buffer
notes: Scans/copies FF00 buffer; branches to 0x429D when done.

label: set_mode_bit_25_from_calt86
notes: Small mode flag helper, writes VV25 bit 7 then CALT 0x0092.

label: set_mode_bit_23_20_from_calt86
notes: Small mode flag helper, toggles VV23 bit 5 then CALT 0x0092.

label: esc_R_select_international_character_set
notes: ESC R n handler: accepts n < 13 and stores n*12 in VVBB for the 0x689C substitution table.

label: apply_international_character_substitution
notes: Searches the base row at 0x689C for #/$/@/bracket-style replacement characters, then applies the VVBB country-row offset.

label: mode_or_geometry_helper
notes: Called from buffer/geometry routines; returns HL/EA-derived values.

label: common_render_or_buffer_helper
notes: High fan-in CALL target in text/graphics region.

label: load_cg_metrics_from_8000_or_8600
notes: Reads F002, selects 0x8000/0x8600 window, uses CALT 0x00B4 and VV:A0 to load EF99/EF9B metrics.

label: fit_glyph_width_and_update_bitmap_pointers
notes: Compares EF97/EF9B/EE88-derived pointers, adjusts EE88 and EF9B, and normalizes VV99 width/count.

label: save_ee88_then_recompute_glyph_source
notes: Saves EE88 to EE8A, calls 0x1DFE, stores computed source pointer back to EE88, then clears a 0x021C-byte work area.

label: compute_glyph_source_pointer_from_ee88
notes: High fan-in companion to 0x1DDF; starts from EE88 and returns a computed pointer in EA/HL.

label: line_feed_vertical_advance_entry
notes: High fan-in line-output routine; clears VV24 bit 0, converts current line spacing EF8B through 0x50EB, then enters the shared vertical advance path at 0x2048.

label: shared_vertical_advance_dispatch
notes: Common LF/FF/ESC J entry after distance setup. Calls 0x105A, chooses render path 0x299D only for a flag combination, otherwise snapshots EE44/EE50/EE4E and jumps to 0x256E.

label: update_page_position_after_advance
notes: Updates vertical/page position state from EF42/EF44/EF3E/EF47 and VV38 flags, storing the resulting current offset at EF11.

label: clear_vv24_bit0
notes: Tiny high-fan-in helper: ANIW VV24,0xFE; RET.

label: esc_J_immediate_forward_feed
notes: ESC J handler: reads one byte, builds positive immediate feed distance in HL, and enters shared feed path at 0x2534.

label: shared_immediate_feed_or_advance_entry
notes: Shared ESC J/ESC j entry: marks VV:C1 bits 0xE0, then normally jumps through 0x1FEA/0x2048 into the 0x256E signed vertical-advance setup.

label: esc_j_immediate_reverse_feed_fx80_compat
notes: ESC j compatibility handler: reads one byte, sets H=0x80 for reverse/negative immediate feed, and enters the same 0x2534 path used by ESC J.

label: setup_signed_vertical_advance_state
notes: Shared advance setup. Stores signed distance in EE7A, normalizes reverse distances into EE86, updates VV38/VV39 flags, snapshots render window state, and later calls 0x2864.

label: process_pending_vertical_advance_distance
notes: Bridge from the render/advance state machine to timed output scheduling. If EE7A has a nonzero distance, stores it in EF40, sets VV38 bit 3, and calls 0x5676 after scheduler-availability checks.

label: initialize_print_geometry_state
notes: Called during reset and adjustment-mode initialization.

label: classify_extended_printable_character
notes: Range ladder over 0xAF-0xDE and 0xF4 using RET/RETS as boolean result; used before selecting font/state tables.

label: setup_8000_render_window_state
notes: Seeds EE46/EE48/EE4A/EE4C/EE4E/EE50 around the 0x8000 work window before the main render loop.

label: inline_calt_dispatch_tail_table
notes: Inline byte table reached by 0x2DC8/0x0FC3 dispatch; disassembles as instructions but is table-like tail data before the 0xFF fill.

label: unused_ff_gap_2de3_3fff
notes: Long 0xFF fill from 0x2DE3 through 0x3FFF.

label: external_prom_signature_or_alt_entry
notes: PBLS-like signature at 0x4000; reset compares 0x0070 against 0x4000 to decide external PROM handling.

label: external_prom_entry_candidate
notes: CALL 0x038B then enters the normal 0x400B input/decode loop. May be external-PROM overlay entry or signature-following code.

label: main_input_decode_loop
notes: Top-level host-byte loop: reads one byte via CALT 0x0080/0x0A0B, calls 0x4038, dispatches controls through 0x6944, or continues to printable output when 0x4038 returns with skip.

label: printable_character_output_path
notes: Entry after 0x4038 classifies a byte as printable; prepares glyph/render state before returning to the 0x400B loop.

label: classify_input_character_and_select_style_state
notes: Normalizes bytes through the 0x6000 identity/remap table, selects current/alternate font state into VV21/VV22/VV1F, and returns with skip for printable bytes.

label: check_line_window_bounds
notes: Compares EE4C+HL against EE5C/EE5E bounds; used by character classifier and test print paths.

label: render_glyph_columns_or_3plane_overlay
notes: Glyph bitmap transform path; uses VV99 width, VV:CF stride, EE88/EE8A pointers, and ORs source columns into destination planes.

label: copy_glyph_columns_to_work_buffer
notes: Glyph transform path that copies packed source columns to the EE88 work buffer, then runs shared shift/merge helpers.

label: render_glyph_columns_variant
notes: Variant of the 0x43DD path with additional shifted-column merge steps.

label: or_shifted_columns_left_into_work_buffer
notes: Shared glyph helper; shifts source column data left by VV:D0 and ORs it into the destination stream.

label: or_shifted_columns_right_into_work_buffer
notes: Shared glyph helper; shifts source column data right by VV:D0 and ORs it into the destination stream.

label: xor_or_mask_shifted_columns_into_work_buffer
notes: Shared glyph helper; applies shifted source bytes with XRAX/OR-style masking into the destination stream.

label: adjust_glyph_width_and_right_edge_metrics
notes: Updates VV99 and EF95/EF97/EF9B after widening/extra-column transforms.

label: trim_or_merge_trailing_glyph_columns
notes: Uses EE88 and VV99 to combine trailing bytes with inverted previous-column masks.

label: set_glyph_work_stride_3
notes: Sets VV:CF to 3 and marks VV2A bit 7 when a non-empty glyph is active.

label: set_glyph_work_stride_4
notes: Sets VV:CF to 4 and marks style bits before packed/nibble expansion.

label: clear_or_pad_expanded_glyph_buffer
notes: Uses 0x1DFE and VV99 to copy/zero expanded glyph columns, then updates EE88 and EF9B.

label: double_or_widen_glyph_columns
notes: Clears a derived work span, duplicates 3-byte columns, and adjusts EF9B; likely double-width or widening support.

label: expand_packed_glyph_by_style_flags
notes: Selects one of several packed-column extraction paths based on VV89 bits and writes expanded bytes through 0x49AD.

label: expand_high_nibble_to_2bit_pairs
notes: Maps input bits 0x80/0x40/0x20/0x10 to output masks 0xC0/0x30/0x0C/0x03; used heavily by packed glyph expansion.

label: condense_or_mask_glyph_columns
notes: Halves/condenses active glyph columns with inverted masks, then updates width and EF95/EF97/EF9B metrics.

label: copy_2byte_glyph_columns_to_3byte_work_rows
notes: Copies two source bytes plus zero padding, or zero plus two source bytes depending VV28 bit 3.

label: write_packed_glyph_nibbles_to_work_buffer
notes: Writes packed nibble/bit groups into destination columns at offsets 0/3/6/9; used for narrow packed glyph paths.

label: gate_array_f001_control_cluster
notes: Cluster of helpers that set/clear F001 bits and related VV flags.

label: clear_f001_bit3
notes: DI; read F001; ANI 0xF7; write F001; clears VV0A bit 0x10.

label: set_f001_bit3
notes: DI; read F001; ORI 0x08; write F001; sets VV0A bit 0x10.

label: txb_send_byte_wait_fst
notes: Waits SKIT FST then writes TXB.

label: read_panel_buttons_debounced
notes: Combines stable PC bits from 0x4EF9 with the 0x4F21 PC08/F2 path; returns 0x01 LINE FEED/AUTO LOAD, 0x02 FORM FEED, and 0x04 ON LINE.

label: read_pc_bits_30_stable
notes: Debounces PC & 0x30 and maps PC bit 0x20 to LINE FEED/AUTO LOAD return bit 0x01 and PC bit 0x10 to FORM FEED return bit 0x02.

label: sample_on_line_pc08_or_f2_button
notes: Uses SKIT F2 plus a PC bit 0x08 wait loop; returns with/without caller skip so 0x4EEA can set ON LINE return bit 0x04.

label: delay_03e8
notes: BC=0x03E8 delay via CALT 0x0090.

label: read_dip_switches_and_panel_pa_bits
notes: Calls table-driven ADC switch reader 0x4F54 twice, stores VV00/VV01, then folds PA bits 0x04/0x08 into VV01.

label: read_adc_switch_table_bits
notes: Walks compact tables at 0x4F96/0x4F9F, selects F002/ANM modes through 0x508D, and builds one switch bit per threshold comparison.

label: sample_vr_adjustment_adc_offsets
notes: Startup and bidirectional-adjustment sampler; averages ADC channels, stores signed offsets at EE28/EE2E-related slots, and clamps them to small ranges.

label: average_adc_sample_127_times
notes: Calls 0x508D 0x7F times for the selected F002/ANM mode and returns the averaged sample.

label: write_f002_select_adc_average_cr0_cr3
notes: Writes F002 from A, configures ANM from B, waits FAD, then averages CR0-CR3 into one ADC value.

label: write_gate_array_f003_from_vv15
notes: Stores A to VV15 and F003.

label: startup_carriage_home_seek_entry
notes: Only traced caller is startup at 0x0340. Branches on PA bit 0x20 HOME candidate, seeds VV61 carriage direction/mode values, calls 0x5253 with short or long distances, and exits through the 0x53E1 post-home path.

label: startup_carriage_home_seek_timed_sequence
notes: Starts with carriage current state 0x547E, walks 10-us-scaled timing tables around 0x7287/0x72AF, samples PA bit 0x20 via 0x5306, pulses the gate-array TM input through 0x0908, and eventually restores hold state 0x546A.

label: sample_home_pa20_during_carriage_delay
notes: Divides a delay interval into thirds, delays, and samples PA bit 0x20 HOME candidate three times while accumulating a small count in D.

label: mechanism_output_state_dispatch
notes: Maps VV37 flags to EFxx state bytes. With uPD7810 skip semantics, VV62!=0 reaches the PB mask 04h drive/hold branch matching the service-manual paper-feed +24 V enable line; VV62=0 indexes the 0x7007 PA/PB output table. For command-feed tail exit, VV37=0x20 selects EF60=00 and reaches 0x5498 to set PB & 04h high/hold.

label: carriage_current_hold_state
notes: Sets PB mask 0x20, PA mask 0x02, and PB mask 0x40, then returns without clearing PB mask 0x20. Service manual says PB5 high is carriage hold current; PA02 appears to fill the manual's PB1/SPDH speed-high selector role but needs schematic confirmation.

label: carriage_current_drive_high_candidate
notes: Sets PB mask 0x20, PA mask 0x02, and PB mask 0x40, then falls through to clear PB mask 0x20 at 0x5491. Candidate high drive-current state.

label: carriage_current_drive_mid_candidate
notes: Sets PB mask 0x20 and PA mask 0x02, clears PB mask 0x40, then falls through to clear PB mask 0x20 at 0x5491. Used by the 0x5253 home-seek timed sequence entry.

label: carriage_current_drive_low_candidate
notes: Sets PB mask 0x20, clears PA mask 0x02, sets PB mask 0x40, then falls through to clear PB mask 0x20 at 0x5491. Candidate lower drive-current state reached by the 0x7007 jump table.

label: arm_timed_mechanism_record
notes: Sets VV37=1, loads a timing/control record via 0x55B1, dispatches initial output state through 0x540D, then arms ETM1/FE1.

label: load_mechanism_timing_record_into_ef49
notes: Copies a 0x17-byte timing/control record from the selected 0x7005/0x7088 table into EF49 and updates EF64/EF51/VV36 according to VV62/VV63/VV61.

label: select_mechanism_timing_record_base
notes: Uses VV62 as a two-byte index into the 0x7001 pointer table, returning the selected timing record base in HL.

label: setup_head_fire_timing_and_data_pointers
notes: Sets VV6D/VV6F flags, derives EF79 from EF64, stores alternate-register source pointer at EF75/EF77, seeds EF70/EF73, and writes F004=0x20.

label: schedule_output_from_ef38_state
notes: Shared timed output scheduler reached from vertical advance and head/setup paths. Copies EF38 state to EF6D, writes F004=0x20, then builds an EF61/EF7C record and reaches 0x558D or the equivalent timed-record arm path.

label: write_f004_head_idle_or_arm_value
notes: Writes 0x20 to F004 after head timing/data pointer setup; same idle/init value written at 0x0487.

label: unused_ff_gap_5794_5fff
notes: Long 0xFF fill from 0x5794 through 0x5FFF.

label: byte_identity_table_00_ff
notes: Full 0x00-0xFF byte table used by 0x4038 to remap host bytes before printable/control classification.

label: service_diagnostic_strings
notes: String/data block: self-test firmware header, data-dump strings, CSF messages, pitch strings, DIP/status menu text.

label: selftest_status_string_pointer_table
notes: Two-column pointer table consumed by 0x75C7; each row supplies left and right status strings, ending with 0000h.

label: selftest_status_selector_strings
notes: Fixed-width DIP/status strings; first byte is a selector ID, followed by FF-delimited printable text.

label: print_style_setup_pointer_table
notes: Pointer table consumed by 0x264F to select print/render setup records at 0x6800-0x682D.

label: print_style_setup_records
notes: Variable-length setup records loaded through the 0x67F0 pointer table into VV88/VV89 and related print state.

label: render_spacing_lookup_overlap
notes: Small lookup consumed by 0x26A0 after masking B with 0x07; overlaps the startup timer table at 0x6834.

label: startup_timer0_table_overlap
notes: Startup table indexed at 0x026E from VV0B and written to TM0; overlaps the 0x682E lookup.

label: extended_glyph_metric_lookup_base
notes: Indexed lookup base consumed by 0x1D3C while deriving glyph metrics/widths for extended character paths.

label: international_character_substitution_table
notes: ESC R/self-test character substitution table consumed by 0x1464; base row lists # $ @ [ \\ ] ^ ` { | } ~.

label: dispatch_control_or_esc_command
notes: Command-table dispatcher. Pushes 0x400B as the loop return, scans byte/target tables, and jumps to matched command handlers.

label: dispatch_esc_command_after_esc
notes: ESC handler from the primary table: reads the next byte with 0x0AB2 and dispatches through the ESC table at 0x699C.

label: primary_control_command_table
notes: Count-prefixed table of 14 primary nonprintable commands: ESC, LF, CR, BEL, BS, HT, VT, FF, SO, SI, DC2, DC3, DC4, CAN.

label: esc_command_table
notes: Count-prefixed table of 62 ESC command bytes and handler addresses, including ESC @, ESC J, ESC K/L/Y/Z, ESC U, ESC C, ESC N/O, and style commands.

label: unused_ff_gap_6a5a_6fff
notes: Long 0xFF fill from 0x6A5A through 0x6FFF.

label: mechanism_timing_record_pointer_table
notes: Two-word pointer table consumed by 0x5635/0x558D; selects timing record bases 0x7005 or 0x7088 from VV62.

label: mechanism_timing_record_table_a
notes: Timing/control record table selected directly by 0x570F or through the 0x7001 pointer table; 0x55B1 copies 0x17-byte records into EF49.

label: mechanism_output_jump_table
notes: Overlapping jump table consumed by 0x540D after indexing from EFxx state byte; targets 0x546A, 0x5474, 0x547E, and 0x5488.

label: mechanism_timing_record_table_b
notes: Alternate timing/control record table selected through the 0x7001 pointer table when VV62 chooses the second record set.

label: paper_feed_short_move_threshold_overlap
notes: Word loaded directly by 0x55D8 from inside the 0x7088 record table. 55DC forces H=00h, so command feed compares the current count against low byte 0x0B; counts below 0x0B take the VV36 mask 04h/EF51=725Fh special path.

label: mechanism_head_timing_word_table
notes: Dense timing/control words following the selected mechanism records; exact sub-record boundaries still need runtime confirmation.

label: cr0_lookup_table
notes: Indexed from ISR at 0x06DF using CR0 range 0xC1-0xEE.

label: paper_feed_timing_list_start_overlap
notes: Word loaded directly by 0x55E4 into EF51 for command-feed counts below the 0x708E threshold; also the first word of the 725Fh timing-list region matching service-manual paper-feed acceleration timing.

label: mechanism_delay_table
notes: Delay/compare table consumed by the 0x5253 PA20 timed mechanism sequence through offsets at 0x7287, 0x729B, and 0x72AD.

label: mechanism_sequence_table
notes: Mechanism sequence/state table consumed by 0x5253; 0x72B3 is also indexed by 0x5719 using a small stride.

label: unclassified_descending_lookup_table
notes: Table-like bytes between mechanism sequence data and render geometry tables; no direct consumer confirmed yet.

label: render_geometry_byte_table
notes: Byte lookup consumed by 0x22DB using VV3A & 7 before downstream geometry table lookups.

label: render_geometry_word_table_7317
notes: Render/layout table consumed by 0x22E4 through the shared 0x23F1-style lookup path.

label: render_geometry_word_table_7341
notes: Render/layout table consumed by 0x220B.

label: render_geometry_word_table_735b
notes: Render/layout table consumed by 0x231F.

label: render_geometry_word_table_736b
notes: Render/layout table consumed by 0x2310.

label: render_geometry_word_table_737b
notes: Render/layout table consumed by 0x2239.

label: render_geometry_word_table_738b
notes: Render/layout table consumed by 0x2248.

label: power_on_panel_mode_dispatch
notes: Dispatches startup panel mode from VV0C: 0x01 draft self-test, 0x02 LQ self-test, 0x03 data dump, and remapped 0x08 bidirectional adjustment.

label: data_dump_grid_printer
notes: Prints rows through the 0x8000 work window, converts nibbles to ASCII, and emits dot placeholders via 0x4038/0x2011.

label: nibble_to_ascii_hex_digit
notes: Converts 0-15 to ASCII hex digit using +7/+0x30 adjustment.

label: print_hex_digit_or_data_dump_cell
notes: Saves HL to EE46, prints one cell through 0x4038, advances by seven bytes, and restores registers.

label: select_print_state_for_character
notes: Selects current/alternate font state into VV21/VV22 based on character range, then checks bounds via 0x4173.

label: draft_self_test_entry
notes: Power-on LINE FEED/AUTO LOAD self-test path; sets VV23 bit 0x04 before common self-test printing.

label: letter_quality_self_test_entry
notes: Power-on FORM FEED self-test path; clears VV23 bit 0x04 before common self-test printing.

label: print_selftest_firmware_header
notes: Prints the 0x6100 L5217B firmware header string, then advances output through 0x2011.

label: print_string_then_output_advance
notes: Calls 0x755D then jumps to 0x2011.

label: print_ff_or_nul_terminated_string
notes: Prints bytes from HL until 0x00/0xFF, using A/C mode and char-output helpers.

label: copy_string_to_ff00_then_print
notes: Copies a source string to 0xFF00 then prints via 0x755D.

label: print_selftest_status_table
notes: Saves current style, builds selected status IDs, prints the two-column 0x61AA table, then restores VV22/VV23/VV24.

label: build_selftest_selected_status_ids
notes: Builds up to twelve selected DIP/status selector IDs in FF00 from VV00/VV01 before the status table is printed.

label: map_country_dip_bits_to_selector_index
notes: Reorders VV00 bits 0-2 into an index used to select one of the country selector IDs.

label: map_font_dip_bits_to_selector_index
notes: Builds the font selector index from VV00 bits 3-4.

label: map_pitch_dip_bits_to_selector_index
notes: Builds the pitch selector index from VV01 bits 6-7.

label: print_status_string_bold_if_selected
notes: Reads a selector-prefixed status string, keeps VV24 bit 0x10 emphasized/bold set only when the selector is present in FF00, then prints the string.

label: print_status_column_spacing
notes: Prints repeated spacing strings from 0x618E unless the high bit says no spacing.

label: data_dump_mode_routine
notes: Prints strings at 0x6116/0x612E/0x613C/0x615E; handles data-dump paper length flow.

label: format_decimal_digits_to_ff00
notes: Decimal formatting helper used by data-dump/service output before printing through the FF00 scratch buffer.

label: bidirectional_adjustment_mode
notes: Prints 0x7A18 'Bi-d Adjustment Mode', VR strings, reads panel/sensor input.

label: print_adjustment_marker_grid
notes: Prints repeated marker string 0x7A3B with output advances; used while rendering bidirectional adjustment samples.

label: print_service_string_then_advance
notes: Prints the FF-delimited string at HL via 0x755D, advances output, then flushes/ends the line.

label: format_adjustment_value_to_ff00
notes: Formats the low byte of HL through 0x77FC into the FF00 scratch string used by the adjustment value display.

label: bidirectional_adjustment_strings
notes: FF-delimited strings and markers: Bi-d Adjustment Mode, VR1, VR2, out of range, space, plus, minus, and the 0xB3 marker.

label: untraced_adjustment_code_fragment
notes: Code-like bytes after the adjustment strings and before the confirmed 0x7AB2 entry; no real entry point confirmed yet.

label: style_typeface_selection_state_machine
notes: Startup/service state machine that updates VV02, VVA4/VVA5, VV1E, and VV23 before falling into the panel output helper.

label: derive_style_selector_from_vv23
notes: Maps VV23 style bits into VV1E selector values 0x80-0x83.

label: apply_style_selector_to_vv23
notes: Maps VV1E low bits back into VV23 style bits 0x20, 0x02, and 0x80 after clearing the previous selector bits.

label: update_panel_select_pa_pb_outputs
notes: Writes PA bit 0 and PB bit 7 depending VV23/VVA5; used during service/adjustment UI setup.

label: unused_ff_gap_7b74_7fff
notes: Long 0xFF fill from 0x7B74 through end of ROM.
